task: In Simple Calendar Pro, create a calendar event in two weeks from today at 1h with the title 'Review session for Campaign' and the description 'We will explore upcoming project milestones. Please bring relevant documents.'. The event should last for 45 mins.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the phone's home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Chrome, Phone, Messages, and a Google search widget. The target app, "Simple Calendar Pro", is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app → | Step 2: Navigate to the date two weeks from today | Step 3: Create a new event at 1:00 AM | Step 4: Fill in title, description, and set duration to 45 mins | Step 5: Save the event

[Decision]
Based on the observation, I am on the home screen and the target app "Simple Calendar Pro" is not directly visible among the shortcuts. To proceed with creating the calendar event, I need to launch the Simple Calendar Pro app. I will execute the action to open it directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The screen displays the Simple Calendar Pro app in a monthly view for October. Today's date is highlighted as Sunday, October 15th. The current month shows various events scheduled on different days. A floating action button with a '+' icon is visible in the bottom right corner.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the date two weeks from today → | Step 3: Create a new event at 1:00 AM | Step 4: Fill in title, description, and set duration to 45 mins | Step 5: Save the event

[Decision]
Based on the observation, today is October 15th. Two weeks from today is October 29th. To proceed with creating the event, I can either select the date first or just initiate the event creation which might default to today's date, but it's safer to select the target date first or proceed to create it. The '+' button is for creating a new event. I will tap the '+' button to start creating the event, and then I can adjust the date to October 29th inside the event creation screen if it doesn't default correctly, or I could tap on the 29th first. Let's tap the '+' button to initiate the creation flow.

[Memory]
Target date: October 29th (two weeks from today, Oct 15th). Target time: 1:00 AM, duration: 45 mins. Title: 'Review session for Campaign', Description: 'We will explore upcoming project milestones. Please bring relevant documents.'.

[Observation]
The screen shows the Simple Calendar Pro app in the monthly view for October. A menu has popped up in the bottom right corner with two options: "Task" and "Event". The date October 29th is visible on the calendar grid.

[Reflection]
The previous plan intended to navigate to the date first, but the previous action tapped the '+' button which brought up a menu to choose between Task and Event. The date selection step hasn't been completed yet, and the interface now requires selecting the event creation type first.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Select "Event" from the creation menu → | Step 3: Select the target date (Oct 29th) or set date/time inside the event creation screen | Step 4: Fill in event details (time, title, description, duration) | Step 5: Save the event

[Decision]
Based on the current screen, the floating menu offers "Task" and "Event". Since the goal is to create a calendar event, I need to choose the "Event" option to proceed to the event creation screen. I will tap the "Event" button with the calendar icon.

[Memory]
Target date: October 29th (two weeks from today, Oct 15th). Target time: 1:00 AM, duration: 45 mins. Title: 'Review session for Campaign', Description: 'We will explore upcoming project milestones. Please bring relevant documents.'.

[Observation]
The screen is on the "New Event" creation page. The keyboard is active. The "Title" input field is at the top, currently empty. Below it are "Location" and "Description" fields. The date is currently set to October 15 (Sun) with a start time of 16:00.

[Reflection]
The previous plan's next step was to select the target date first, but since I am already on the event creation screen and the keyboard is active, it's more efficient to fill in the text fields first (like Title) before adjusting the date and time.

[Replan]
Step 1-2: Select "Event" ✓ | Step 3: Fill in event details (Title, Description) → | Step 4: Set date to Oct 29th, time to 1:00 AM, duration to 45 mins | Step 5: Save the event

[Decision]
The current step in the updated plan is to fill in the event details, starting with the title. The "Title" field is at the top of the screen. I need to type the required title 'Review session for Campaign' into this field.

[Memory]
Target date: October 29th (two weeks from today, Oct 15th). Target time: 1:00 AM, duration: 45 mins. Title: 'Review session for Campaign' (currently typing). Description: 'We will explore upcoming project milestones. Please bring relevant documents.'. Current default date is Oct 15th, needs to be changed later.

The screen is on the "New Event" page. The title "Review session for Campaign" has been successfully entered. Below the title, there are fields for "Location" and "Description". The keyboard is currently visible on the screen. The default date is still October 15 (Sun).

Step 1-2: Select "Event" ✓ | Step 3: Fill in event details (Title ✓, Description →) | Step 4: Set date to Oct 29th, time to 1:00 AM, duration to 45 mins | Step 5: Save the event

According to the current step in the plan, the next detail to fill in is the description. The "Description" field is visible below the Location field. I need to type the required description text 'We will explore upcoming project milestones. Please bring relevant documents.' into this field.
